region_of_origin: Middle East / Central Asia (served in Singapore context)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for baked beef-filled dough parcels (manti/mantije). 'Dumpling food product' (FOODON_03315091) is the closest defensible parent class; exact match unavailable.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0EJW covers meat-filled pasta and dumplings as a category; mantije is a baked variant not explicitly enumerated. Used as closest defensible FoodEx2 anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT clinical food concept found for mantije or manti dumplings; left null to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of mantije consists of approximately 5 to 6 baked dough parcels weighing around 150 g, served with a yogurt dipping sauce on the side.

nutrient_source.title: Mantije (Beef) — Singapore HPB Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Mantije (Beef), cr_id G00010158. All core macros and secondary nutrients are taken from this record at confidence 0.95. Saturated fat is estimated from USDA FoodData Central beef mince data (USDA SR Legacy #13961) as HPB does not report saturated fat breakdown for this entry; this is documented in notes.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for mantije (baked beef-filled dough parcels). GI estimated at 47 (low) by analogy with meat-filled pasta / ravioli (GI ~39–50, University of Sydney GI Database). The very low carbohydrate content (4.19 g/100 g) means glycaemic impact is minimal regardless of GI category. GL per 150 g serving = (47 × 4.19 × 1.5) / 100 = 2.96 g, confirming negligible glycaemic load.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 369 mg per 100 g; a 150 g serving delivers approximately 554 mg sodium, representing ~25% of the 2,000 mg daily limit recommended for hypertensive individuals.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit mantije to a single small serving, request reduced salt in preparation, and avoid or minimise the accompanying yogurt-garlic sauce to prevent cumulative sodium intake from exceeding daily targets.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low carbohydrate content (4.19 g/100 g) and high protein (18.48 g/100 g) support post-prandial glycaemic stability; estimated GI of 47 (low) and GL of ~3 per serving confirm minimal glycaemic impact.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Mantije is suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes in moderate portions; the high protein and low carbohydrate profile supports glycaemic control, but portion size should be monitored to avoid cumulative refined carbohydrate load from the dough.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~2.3 g/100 g estimated) from ground beef and cholesterol (52 mg/100 g) are relevant for LDL management; sodium at 369 mg/100 g adds cardiovascular risk through blood pressure elevation.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume mantije occasionally and in small portions, prioritising lean beef preparations, requesting reduced salt, and balancing the meal with high-fibre vegetables to offset the saturated fat and sodium burden.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Ground beef is a significant source of arachidonic acid, a precursor to pro-inflammatory eicosanoids; regular consumption may amplify joint inflammation in RA patients.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should limit or avoid mantije due to the pro-inflammatory arachidonic acid content of ground beef; substituting with chicken or plant-based fillings and requesting olive oil-based preparation would reduce inflammatory load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Mantije (Beef) is a baked stuffed dough dish of Middle Eastern and Central Asian origin, widely served in Singapore's multicultural food landscape. It consists of small parcels of hand-stretched wheat flour dough filled with seasoned ground beef, onion, and spices, then baked until golden brown. The dish is characteristically served with a yogurt dipping sauce, often flavoured with garlic. The texture combines a slightly crisp outer dough shell with a juicy, savoury beef filling. Nutritionally, mantije is notable for its high protein content (18.48 g/100 g) and low carbohydrate profile (4.19 g/100 g), making it one of the more protein-dense baked dough dishes available. Fat is moderate at 6.0 g/100 g. Sodium is elevated at 369 mg/100 g, which is a primary clinical concern for hypertensive individuals. The estimated glycaemic index of 47 (low) and glycaemic load of approximately 3 per 150 g serving indicate minimal impact on blood glucose, making it broadly suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes. Cardiovascular considerations centre on saturated fat from beef and the sodium content. The dish is halal-certified in most Singapore preparations. Mantije is related to the broader manti dumpling family found across Turkey, Central Asia, and the Levant, and is sometimes transliterated as 'manti' or 'mantı' in English-language menus.

confidence_reason: Core nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010158) at confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from USDA SR Legacy beef mince data as HPB does not report fatty acid breakdown; this is the only adapted field. All other values are exact from the primary approved source.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (2.3 g/100 g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry #13961 (Ground beef, 85% lean / 15% fat, cooked) proportionally adjusted for the mixed dish composition; HPB does not report saturated fat for this entry. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, micronutrient panel, and moisture data for this traditional baked beef dumpling preparation are not available in the HPB Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as a composite dish entry as of 2026. 3. GI value of 47 is an analogous estimate based on meat-filled pasta / ravioli from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for mantije exists in the approved GI literature. 4. The input record listed 'rheumatoid_arthritis' as a health suitability condition; this has been preserved in health_context.additional_conditions. 5. Halal status is assumed based on typical Singapore preparation context; individual vendor verification is recommended.
